summary: W3 application seam PASS: layering inward, no scheduleQueue, single delta-writer (inline flush gone). Smells for W4: partial seam (3 inline writes bypass command) + no facade/non-mirrored layout.

findings.claim: Caller still owns direct DB writes outside the application command — partial-orchestration seam.
findings.evidence: runScheduleAndPersist delegates persistDelta/deleteTasksWhere/backfillRollingAnchor/dbNow/clockNow to the command but keeps three inline writes around it: db('task_instances').update({unscheduled:1}) at 886 (rollback-survival, on db not trx), trx('task_instances').update(driftFields) drift CASE-update at 911, and the user_config schedule_cache upsert at 1777-1782. The slice writes the DB both through and around the command, so the command's 'sole persist seam' docstring is aspirational not enforced.
findings.fix: W4/H7: lift remaining writes behind the command (persistCache/flagUnscheduled/persistDrift). Document the 886 db-not-trx rollback-survival semantic in the port contract before moving it; do not silently fold a non-trx write into a trx-scoped primitive.

findings.claim: Scheduler application layer diverges from sibling slice shape: no commands/ subdir, no facade.js.
findings.evidence: slices/task/application and slices/user-config/application use a commands/ (+queries/) subdir AND a facade.js guarded by eslint.boundaries.config.js rules. Scheduler puts RunScheduleCommand.js directly under application/ with no facade; runSchedule.js imports the command class directly via require('../slices/scheduler/application/RunScheduleCommand'). Nothing prevents a future caller reaching past the application layer into adapters.
findings.fix: W4: add slices/scheduler/facade.js as the single entry + move command under application/commands/ to mirror task/user-config, plus the per-slice boundary eslint rule. OR record an ADR for the intentional single-command/no-query divergence (refer authoring to abby).

findings.claim: Module-level command singleton is stateless and Cloud-Run scale-out safe.
findings.evidence: _runScheduleCommand = new RunScheduleCommand() holds only an injected clock + repositoryFactory (pure factories, no per-request mutable state). No instance-local state crosses requests.

findings.claim: S4/S6 scheduleQueue invariant holds — the command persists, it does not self-trigger.
findings.evidence: Zero live scheduleQueue/enqueueScheduleRun references in application/; the only 3 grep hits are the invariant's own doc comments. Guarded by the golden-master require-closure assert.

findings.claim: Per-slice scheduler boundary eslint rule absent — confirmed expected for W4.
findings.evidence: eslint.boundaries.config.js has facade-boundary rules for calendar/weather/task/ai-enrichment/user-config but no scheduler entry. Matches the stated expectation that scheduler boundary enforcement lands in W4.
findings.fix: W4: add the scheduler facade-boundary rule once the facade exists.

findings.claim: Drift CASE-WHEN UPDATE + safety-net flag are correctness-sensitive; SQL binding/chunk correctness is out of cookie's column.
findings.evidence: The batched CASE-WHEN drift update (902-911) and the unscheduled safety-net flag are correctness-sensitive scheduler writes; architecture is sound but per-statement binding-count/chunk-vs-max_allowed_packet is a code-correctness concern.

refers.why: verify drift CASE-update binding count + chunk-size vs max_allowed_packet correctness (runSchedule.js 902-911)
